DatabaseAuthType.description:
Database authentication types.

- SQL: Standard username/password authentication
- WINDOWS_INTEGRATED: Windows Active Directory authentication (MSSQL only)
DatabaseAuthType.title: DatabaseAuthType

DatabaseConnectionConfig.description:
Database connection configuration.

Supports Oracle, MSSQL, PostgreSQL, MySQL.
Secrets are referenced using ${secrets.xxx} pattern.

MSSQL Windows Integrated Auth:
    For local development with Windows AD authentication, set:
    - auth_type: "windows_integrated"
    - username/password can be omitted (uses current Windows session)
    
    Connection string pattern:
    mssql+pyodbc://@SERVER/DATABASE?trusted_connection=yes&driver=ODBC+Driver+18+for+SQL+Server

auth_type.description: Authentication type (sql or windows_integrated for MSSQL)

driver.description: ODBC driver name for MSSQL (default: Driver 17, pre-installed on Databricks)
driver.title: Driver

password.description: Password (use ${secrets.xxx} for secret reference). Optional for windows_integrated auth.
password.title: Password

username.description: Username (use ${secrets.xxx} for secret reference). Optional for windows_integrated auth.
username.title: Username

DatabaseConnectionConfig.title: DatabaseConnectionConfig

DatabaseOptionsConfig.description:
Database-specific options.

Reference: https://dlthub.com/docs/dlt-ecosystem/verified-sources/sql_database

Oracle-specific notes:
    - detect_precision_hints: Critical for Oracle NUMBER type handling
    - defer_table_reflect: Enables lazy schema loading for performance
    - parallelize: Enables parallel table extraction for Oracle

backend.description: Backend for reading data (pyarrow recommended for nullable types)
backend.title: Backend

defer_table_reflect.description: Lazy schema loading - reflects schema only when needed
defer_table_reflect.title: Defer Table Reflect

detect_precision_hints.description: Detect precision hints for Oracle NUMBER types (critical for Oracle)
detect_precision_hints.title: Detect Precision Hints

parallelize.description: Enable parallel extraction for database sources (useful for Oracle)
parallelize.title: Parallelize

DatabaseOptionsConfig.title: DatabaseOptionsConfig

DatabricksConnectionConfig.description:
Databricks source connection configuration for Reverse ETL.

Uses databricks-sql-connector via SQLAlchemy for reading Delta tables
from a remote Databricks workspace.

Reference: https://docs.databricks.com/dev-tools/python-sql-connector.html

access_token.description: Personal access token or service principal token. Use ${secrets.xxx} pattern.
access_token.title: Access Token

catalog.description: Unity Catalog name to read from
catalog.title: Catalog

client_id.description: OAuth client ID for service principal auth. Use ${secrets.xxx}.
client_id.title: Client Id

client_secret.description: OAuth client secret for service principal auth. Use ${secrets.xxx}.
client_secret.title: Client Secret

http_path.description: HTTP path for SQL warehouse or cluster (e.g., '/sql/1.0/warehouses/abc123')
http_path.title: Http Path

schema_name.description: Default schema within catalog (optional, can specify per table)
schema_name.title: Schema Name

DatabricksConnectionConfig.title: DatabricksConnectionConfig

DatabricksSourceConfig.description:
Databricks source configuration for Reverse ETL.

File location: configs/sources/databricks/{source_name}.yaml

Use cases:
    - Reverse ETL to Oracle (push aggregated data back to operational DB)
    - Reverse ETL to MSSQL (sync curated data to legacy systems)
    - Data export to external partners
    - Push ML predictions or KPIs to transactional systems

Implementation: Uses dlt's sql_database source with databricks-sql-connector.
The framework generates SQLAlchemy connection string for Databricks.
DatabricksSourceConfig.title: DatabricksSourceConfig

DateGranularity.description:
Date granularity for folder partitioning.

Based on GRM Germany FileRepositoryDateGranularity pattern.
DateGranularity.title: DateGranularity

FileCompression.description: Supported compression types for file output.
FileCompression.title: FileCompression

FileFormat.description:
Supported file formats for filesystem source/destination.

dlt NATIVE READERS:
    - CSV:     read_csv() via pandas, or read_csv_duckdb() for better performance
    - PARQUET: read_parquet() via pyarrow
    - JSONL:   read_jsonl() - line-delimited JSON

FRAMEWORK-PROVIDED TRANSFORMERS:
    - JSON:  Single JSON files - framework provides read_json() transformer

REQUIRES CUSTOM TRANSFORMERS (contact central team):
    - AVRO:  Apache Avro format (requires fastavro)
    - XLSX:  Excel files (requires openpyxl)

PERFORMANCE NOTES:
    - Use read_csv_duckdb() instead of read_csv() for better CSV performance
    - Set use_pyarrow=True for Parquet/CSV to enable zero-copy Arrow format
    - Increase chunk_size for large files (10,000+ recommended for production)
FileFormat.title: FileFormat

FilesystemConnectionConfig.description:
Filesystem connection configuration.

Supports multiple protocols: local, Azure ADLS, S3, GCS, SFTP.

account_name.description: Storage account name (Azure) or access key ID (AWS)
account_name.title: Account Name

protocol.description: Filesystem protocol (az, s3, gs, file, sftp)

FilesystemConnectionConfig.title: FilesystemConnectionConfig

FilesystemFileOptionsConfig.description:
File format, compression, and performance options for filesystem operations.

Note: file_glob moved to FilesystemPathConfig for source reading.
This class handles format, compression, performance tuning, and output file naming.

PERFORMANCE TUNING:
    - chunk_size: Number of rows/records per batch. Larger = faster but more memory.
    - use_pyarrow: Enable zero-copy Arrow format for Parquet/CSV (faster for large files).
    - use_duckdb_csv_reader: Use DuckDB instead of pandas for CSV (faster).

RECOMMENDED SETTINGS BY FILE SIZE:
    - Small files (<10MB): chunk_size=1000, defaults are fine
    - Medium files (10-100MB): chunk_size=10000
    - Large files (>100MB): chunk_size=50000, use_pyarrow=True

chunk_size.description:
Number of rows/records to process per batch.
Larger values = better throughput but higher memory usage.
Recommended: 1000 (small files), 10000 (medium), 50000+ (large)
chunk_size.title: Chunk Size

compression.description: Compression type (snappy, gzip, lz4, zstd, none)

file_name_pattern.title: File Name Pattern

format.description: File format (parquet, avro, json, jsonl, csv)

max_file_size_mb.description: Maximum output file size in MB before splitting
max_file_size_mb.title: Max File Size Mb

use_duckdb_csv_reader.description:
Use DuckDB reader for CSV files instead of pandas.
DuckDB is typically faster and handles compression better.
Set to False only if you need specific pandas CSV options.
use_duckdb_csv_reader.title: Use Duckdb Csv Reader

use_pyarrow.description:
Use PyArrow RecordBatch format instead of Python dicts.
Enables zero-copy operations for better performance with large files.
Only effective for Parquet and CSV (with DuckDB reader).
Set to True when loading large files (>100MB) to Databricks.
use_pyarrow.title: Use Pyarrow

FilesystemFileOptionsConfig.title: FilesystemFileOptionsConfig

FilesystemIncrementalConfig.description:
Incremental scanning configuration for filesystem sources.

Maps to dlt's native incremental loading capabilities:

| track_by       | dlt Mapping                                  | Use Case                          |
|----------------|----------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| file_modified  | dlt.sources.incremental("modification_date") | Files touched when new data added |
| file_name      | dlt.sources.incremental("file_name")         | Alphabetical file name ordering   |
| file_url       | dlt.sources.incremental("file_url")          | Full path ordering (see below)    |
| folder_date    | Framework parses date from folder path       | GRM-style partitioned folders     |

FILE_URL INCREMENTAL EXPLAINED:

file_url is the FULL PATH to each file and is dlt's PRIMARY KEY for files.
Since paths sort lexicographically, date-partitioned paths sort chronologically:

    az://container/data/2024/01/28/orders.parquet  (earlier)
    az://container/data/2024/01/29/orders.parquet  (later)
    az://container/data/2024/01/30/orders.parquet  (latest)

Using file_url incremental with initial_value="2024/01/28/":
    - dlt will only return files with file_url >= initial_value
    - After first run, state stores the max file_url processed
    - Next run only processes files > last processed file_url

This is ideal for date-partitioned folders where folder names ARE the dates.

FOLDER_DATE vs FILE_URL:
    - folder_date: Framework parses "{year}/{month}/{day}" pattern into date
    - file_url: Uses raw path string comparison (simpler, more flexible)
    
For most date-partitioned sources, folder_date is preferred because it
explicitly understands the date semantics. Use file_url when you need
raw path-based ordering without date parsing.

initial_value.description:
Initial value for first run. Format depends on track_by:
- file_modified: ISO datetime '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z'
- file_name: File name prefix 'data_2024'
- file_url: Path prefix '2024/01/01/' (matches folder_pattern)
- folder_date: Folder path '2024/01/01' (matches folder_pattern format)
initial_value.title: Initial Value

row_order.description:
Sort order for file processing (dlt native support):
- asc: Process oldest files first (default for most cases)
- desc: Process newest files first
row_order.title: Row Order

track_by.description:
How to track incremental state:
- file_modified: Track by file modification timestamp (dlt native)
- file_name: Track by file name ordering (dlt native)
- file_url: Track by full file path/URL (dlt native)
- folder_date: Parse date from folder path pattern (framework enhanced)
track_by.title: Track By

FilesystemIncrementalConfig.title: FilesystemIncrementalConfig

FilesystemPathConfig.description:
Filesystem path configuration with flexible partitioning support.

Supports TWO patterns:

1. MULTI-FOLDER PARTITIONED (GRM Germany style):
   Base: file-repository/Trading/Oracle
   Folders: {year}/{month}/{day}/ or year={year}/month={month}/day={day}/
   Files: *.parquet
   Result: file-repository/Trading/Oracle/2024/01/28/*.parquet

2. SINGLE-FOLDER with date-in-filename:
   Base: landing-zone/partner-feed
   Folders: null (no folder partitioning)
   Files: data_{year}{month}{day}*.csv
   Result: landing-zone/partner-feed/data_20240128*.csv

MULTI-LEVEL FOLDER STRUCTURES:
   The base_path handles any static folder hierarchy BEFORE date folders:
   
   Example 1: germany/hr/advisor/2026/01/01/
     base_path: "germany/hr/advisor"
     folder_pattern: "{year}/{month}/{day}/"
   
   Example 2: uk/2026/01/01/
     base_path: "uk"
     folder_pattern: "{year}/{month}/{day}/"
   
   Example 3: data/region=emea/country=de/2026/01/01/
     base_path: "data/region=emea/country=de"
     folder_pattern: "{year}/{month}/{day}/"

Path pattern variables:
    - {year}, {month}, {day}, {hour}: Date components
    - {source_name}, {table_name}: Runtime substitution
    - {timestamp}, {load_id}: Load-time values

date_granularity.description: Date granularity for folder partitioning or filename date extraction

file_glob.description:
Glob pattern for matching files. Supports date variables for single-folder pattern.
Examples: '*.parquet', 'data_{year}{month}{day}*.csv', '**/*.json'
file_glob.title: File Glob

folder_pattern.description:
Folder partition pattern with date variables. Set to null for single-folder sources where date is in filename.
Examples: '{year}/{month}/{day}/', 'year={year}/month={month}/day={day}/' (Hive-style)
folder_pattern.title: Folder Pattern

partition_style.description: Partition folder naming style (numeric or hive_style)

FilesystemPathConfig.title: FilesystemPathConfig

FilesystemSourceConfig.description:
Filesystem source configuration for reading from partitioned folders.

File location: configs/sources/filesystem/{source_name}.yaml

Use cases:

    - External data feeds with date-partitioned folders
    - Historical file migrations

RestApiResourceDefaultsConfig.description:
Shared defaults for all REST API resources.

Use this to avoid repeating common settings across 50+ endpoints.
Individual resource configs can override any of these defaults.

Example YAML:
    resource_defaults:
      primary_key: "id"
      write_disposition: "merge"
      params:
        per_page: 100
      pagination:
        type: "header_link"
        links_next_key: "next"

data_selector.description: Default JSONPath to data array in response
data_selector.title: Data Selector

params.description: Default query parameters for all endpoints
params.title: Params

primary_key.description: Default primary key for all resources. Set explicitly if needed.
primary_key.title: Primary Key

write_disposition.description: Default write disposition for all resources

RestApiResourceDefaultsConfig.title: RestApiResourceDefaultsConfig

RestApiSourceConfig.description:
REST API source configuration.

File location: configs/sources/rest_api/{source_name}.yaml

Features:
    - resource_defaults: Shared settings applied to all resources
    - resources: Individual endpoint configurations
    - Rate limiting support via connection.rate_limit

resource_defaults.description: Default settings applied to all resources. Individual resource configs can override these values.

resources.description: API endpoints to extract (can also be in separate resource files)
resources.title: Resources
